channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from Maputo, Mozambique's capital, shaped by a modernist architecture
item.description: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.content:encoded: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: On a flight home, a stranger helped her understand what came next
item.description: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.content:encoded: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kwerel

item.title: 'Coreano Hermano': Ahead of Mexico vs. South Korea, it's all love between the fans
item.description: It's an unusual sight before a World Cup match: Supporters of opposing teams partying together, calling each other "brother," some even hoping for a draw. But fans say they're far from rivals.
item.content:encoded:
It's an unusual sight before a World Cup match: Supporters of opposing teams partying together, calling each other "brother," some even hoping for a draw. But fans say they're far from rivals.

(Image credit: Karla Gachet for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: 'Spider-Noir' is the best TV superhero series since 'The Penguin'
item.description: A new Prime Video series imagines Spiderman as a gumshoe of the 1930s — but with superpowers. Spider-Noir represents one of the boldest performances of Nicolas Cage's entire risk-taking career.
item.content:encoded: A new Prime Video series imagines Spiderman as a gumshoe of the 1930s — but with superpowers. Spider-Noir represents one of the boldest performances of Nicolas Cage's entire risk-taking career.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'The Lost Founder' profiles a brilliant lawyer who helped craft the Constitution
item.description: Jesse Wegman's book tells the story of James Wilson, a largely forgotten founding father who lived a colorful life and died as a Supreme Court justice on the run from the law and creditors.
item.content:encoded: Jesse Wegman's book tells the story of James Wilson, a largely forgotten founding father who lived a colorful life and died as a Supreme Court justice on the run from the law and creditors.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Faith's role in U.S. politics 'requires humility,' not certainty, says Sen. Warnock
item.description: Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Senator Raphael Warnock of Georgia about his new book, "The Crooked Places Made Straight: Reflections on the Moral Meaning of America."
item.content:encoded:
Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Senator Raphael Warnock of Georgia about his new book, "The Crooked Places Made Straight: Reflections on the Moral Meaning of America."

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: DMV artist turns belts into a conversation about discipline
item.description: Multidisciplinary artist Lex Marie has gone viral on TikTok and Instagram for her artwork confronting discipline within Black households.
item.content:encoded: Multidisciplinary artist Lex Marie has gone viral on TikTok and Instagram for her artwork confronting discipline within Black households.
item.dc:creator: Nia Dumas

item.title: Soccer Edition: Watch World Cup highlights from across the NPR Network
item.description: The 2026 World Cup is playing out in communities across the country. Journalists from NPR and its member stations are in your city — capturing the excitement and asking the important questions.
item.content:encoded: The 2026 World Cup is playing out in communities across the country. Journalists from NPR and its member stations are in your city — capturing the excitement and asking the important questions.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: As the U.S. turns 250, this historian has blunt advice: 'America has to grow up'
item.description: In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.
item.content:encoded:
In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.

(Image credit: Lisa Lake)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: If you plan to catch up on reading this summer, start with these 3 books
item.description: Summer is the perfect time to go back to great books that whizzed by in spring, including The Family Man, by James Lasdun, The Hill, by Harriet Clark and A Beautiful Loan, by Mary Costello
item.content:encoded: Summer is the perfect time to go back to great books that whizzed by in spring, including The Family Man, by James Lasdun, The Hill, by Harriet Clark and A Beautiful Loan, by Mary Costello
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan
